title: unify config locations
body:
/kind feature

**Description**
Right now, _podman_ reads configs from various locations.

**containers.conf**
1. `/usr/share/containers/`
2. `/etc/containers/`
3. `$HOME/.config/containers/`

**storage.conf**
1. `/etc/containers/`
2. `$HOME/.config/containers/`

**registry.conf**
1. `/etc/containers/`
2. `/etc/containers/registries.d/`
3. `$HOME/.config/containers/`

**policy.json**
1. `/etc/containers/`
2. `$HOME/.config/containers/`

**seccomp.json**
1. `/usr/share/containers/`
2. `/etc/containers/`

**cni/*.conflist**
1. `/etc/cni/net.d/`
2. `$HOME/.config/cni/net.d/`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Would be fine to have this unified
1. `/usr/share/containers/`
2. `/etc/containers/`
3. `$HOME/.config/containers/`

Plus some dot-d-directories of course.

comments.body:
Seccomp should already use `/etc/containers/seccomp.json` if present.

I do not think we should change the CNI directories `/etc/cni/net.d/` and `$HOME/.config/cni/net.d/`
@rhatdan Did you patched `containers.conf` to read from `containers.conf.d` directories?

Thanks, I've updated my initial post. Here another design:

|                           | containers.conf | storage.conf | registry.conf | policy.json | seccomp.json | cni/*.conflist |
|---------------------------|-----------------|--------------|---------------|-------------|--------------|----------------|
| /usr/share/containers/    | 1               | 0            | 0             | 0           | 1            | 0              |
| /etc/containers/          | 1               | 1            | 1             | 1           | 1            | 0              |
| $HOME/.config/containers/ | 1               | 1            | 1             | 1           | 0            | 0              |
| /etc/cni/net.d/           | 0               | 0            | 0             | 0           | 0            | 1              |
| $HOME/.config/cni/net.d/  | 0               | 0            | 0             | 0           | 0            | 1              |
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

registries.conf can also read from $HOME/.confing/containers/registries.conf.d. Note 100 percent sure about containers.conf.

While it would be nice to have consistency for all configs, some of them grew historically with requirements from other tools.  I'd be terrified of changing the loading behavior and breaking consumers.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think we satisfied the things we could in this issue, but we will not unify all of the config files. Reopen if you think there is more work to be done.
